error_messages.testDateTimeInstance:
java.text.ParseException: Unable to parse: 1/1/70 12:00 AM
	at org.apache.commons.lang3.time.FastDateParser.parse(FastDateParser.java:68)
	at org.apache.commons.lang3.time.FastDateFormat.parse(FastDateFormat.java:89)
	at org.apache.commons.lang3.time.FastDateFormatTest.testDateTimeInstance(FastDateFormatTest.java:36)

Expected: Parsed Date should not be null.
Actual:   null returned from parse() without throwing an exception (silent failure),
          then the null-check assertion in the test caught the issue.
          Root cause: FastDateParser.parse(String,ParsePosition) returns null
          without setting the error index, and the regex in buildRegex() does
          not escape literal characters properly.
error_messages.testFormat:
java.text.ParseException: Unable to parse: 2023-07-15
	at org.apache.commons.lang3.time.FastDateParser.parse(FastDateParser.java:68)
	at org.apache.commons.lang3.time.FastDateFormat.parse(FastDateFormat.java:89)
	at org.apache.commons.lang3.time.FastDateFormatTest.testFormat(FastDateFormatTest.java:22)
	at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)

Expected: parse("2023-07-15") to return a non-null Date with the correct year/month/day.
Actual:   ParseException thrown because regex special character '-' was not escaped
          in buildRegex(), causing the compiled Pattern to fail matching.

covered_classes.doc:
FastDateFormat is a fast and thread-safe version of SimpleDateFormat.
 * It delegates formatting to FastDatePrinter and parsing to FastDateParser.

covered_classes.doc:
FastDateParser is a thread-safe date parser.
 * It uses regex-based parsing to achieve higher performance than SimpleDateFormat.

covered_methods.doc:
Initializes the internal regex pattern for parsing.
     * This method calls buildRegex to construct the pattern string.

Converts the date format pattern to a regex string.
     * Handles common Java date pattern letters (y, M, d, H, m, s).

Parses the given date string using the stored pattern.
     * Returns the parsed Date object.
     *
     * @param source the date string to parse
     * @return the parsed Date
     * @throws ParseException if the string cannot be parsed

Parses the date string at the given position.
     * This method calls buildRegex internally to match date fields.
